title: Yeast Transformation Protocol
author: Marijn Ceelen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7jkhkkw

steps.title: Make a transformation mix consisting of
steps.instruction: Make a transformation mix consisting of: %s %s

steps.title: Centrifuge the 1
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 1.5 mL tubes with 100 µL of yeast competent cells for 2 minutes at 5000x g and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 360 µL of the transformation mix into the 1
steps.instruction: Add 360 µL of the transformation mix into the 1.5 mL with the cells and vortex trhoughly.

steps.title: Heat-shock the solution at 42 ºC for 40 minutes
steps.instruction: Heat-shock the solution at 42 ºC for 40 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifige the cells for 2 minutes...
steps.instruction: Centrifige the cells for 2 minutes at 5000x g and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the cells in 1 mL of YPD medium
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in 1 mL of YPD medium. Divide the volume into two different 1.5 mL tubes.

steps.title: Leave cells to recover for 1
steps.instruction: Leave cells to recover for 1.5 hours at 30 ºC and 300 rpm.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells for 2 minutes at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells for 2 minutes at 5000x g and decant supernatant, leaving a bit of the medium inside the tubes.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in the resting medium...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in the resting medium and plate 50 µL onto a SC plate without the amino acid used as a selection marker.

steps.title: Incubate the cells for 3 days at 30 ºC
steps.instruction: Incubate the cells for 3 days at 30 ºC.
